metadata.title: Reading the Skip Rate: Why a Low Skip Rate Means the Filter Is Failing

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The claim that an 80 percent apply rate is evidence of failure inverted everything I assumed about productivity. I want to internalize the skip-rate bands. Claude, help me (1) take a tracker log of roles evaluated, applied, and skipped and compute the skip rate, (2) classify it into the too-loose, borderline, healthy, or starved band, and (3) compare two weeks with the same evaluation count but different skip rates and estimate the freed networking hours each produced.

props.command: The claim that an 80 percent apply rate is evidence of failure inverted everything I assumed about productivity. I want to internalize the skip-rate bands. Claude, help me (1) take a tracker log of roles evaluated, applied, and skipped and compute the skip rate, (2) classify it into the too-loose, borderline, healthy, or starved band, and (3) compare two weeks with the same evaluation count but different skip rates and estimate the freed networking hours each produced.
props.segment: Reading the Skip Rate: Why a

beats.name: Title
beats.narration_text: An AI analysis praises a job searcher for applying to 80 percent of the roles they evaluated. The praise is a warning sign.

beats.name: WeeklyTracker
beats.narration_text: The weekly tracker: 30 roles evaluated. 24 applied. 6 skipped. Skip rate: 20 percent. The engine read it as highly productive — covering lots of ground.

beats.name: TheQuestion
beats.narration_text: Applying to more roles should mean more progress. Here is the case where an 80 percent apply rate is evidence the engine has failed. Why?

beats.name: TheDial
beats.narration_text: The pipeline tracker produces one number that tells you whether the whole method is operating: the skip rate. The target is at least 50 percent.

beats.name: DialBands
beats.narration_text: Below 40 percent: the filter is too loose. 40 to 50: borderline. Above 50 with steady applies: healthy. Above 85: the funnel is starved. Four bands. One reading required before anything else.

beats.name: SkipIsAction
beats.narration_text: The skip is a first-class action. A tracker that logs only applications is missing half the data about whether the filter works. Skipping is not absence. It is a decision.

beats.name: FilterCollapse
beats.narration_text: A skip rate below 50 percent means the filter found almost everything worth applying to — which means it found nothing. The targeted two-hour block has quietly re-expanded.

beats.name: VolumeInstinct
beats.narration_text: The counter going up is not progress. It is the volume instinct reclaiming the time the filter just saved. Busy and unfiltered is exactly the state the whole engine exists to end.

beats.name: TwoWeeks
beats.narration_text: Two weeks. Same candidate. Same engine. Week A: 30 evaluated, 6 skipped, 20 percent — filter failing band. Week B: 30 evaluated, 18 skipped, 60 percent — healthy band. Week B generated 2 referrals from freed networking hours. Week A generated 0. Illustrative numbers.

beats.name: Endcard
beats.narration_text: The low skip rate felt more productive. The high skip rate was. Read the dial before you read anything else.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The counter going up is not progress. It is the volume instinct reclaiming the time the filter just saved. Busy and unfiltered is exactly the state the whole engine exists to end. Two weeks. Same candidate. Same engine. Week A: 30 evaluated, 6 skipped, 20 percent — filter failing band. Week B: 30 evaluated, 18 skipped, 60 percent — healthy band. Week B generated 2 referrals from freed networking hours. Week A generated 0. Illustrative numbers.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.
